channel.title: Just In
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Australian Broadcasting Corporation. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: Book tells story of Timorese asylum seekers' daring escape to Darwin
item.description: Jose da Costa's escape from Indonesian-occupied East Timor in 1995 has been detailed in a new book launched at the Northern Territory Writers Festival this weekend.
item.dc:creator: Tilda Colling

media:group.media:description: Jose da Costa sought refuge in Australia after fleeing the Indonesian occupation of East Timor in the 1990s.

item.title: How Mr Skelly offers 'vulnerable connection' to love, death and grief
item.description: An animation series tackling themes of grief, regret, love and gratitude has attracted an online fanbase of millions — and reflects the artist's search for peace and beauty.
item.dc:creator: Selina Bryan

media:group.media:description: Mr Skelly is a character created by animator Christian Watson.

item.title: Man charged with arson after house destroyed north of Brisbane
item.description: Police will allege a 36-year-old man used accelerant to start a fire at a house in Clontarf yesterday.

media:group.media:description: Emergency services were called to a fire at Clontarf on Saturday afternoon.

item.title: A planning rule wouldn't bend so the lights at this Canberra oval had to
item.description: If the six light poles skirting Mauka Oval look like they're on a lean, it's because they are. The 47-metre-high poles slant inwards because otherwise they couldn't be approved under an unusual Canberra planning rule.
item.dc:creator: Lish Fejer

media:group.media:description: The Maunka Oval light poles are curved in order to comply with a planning rule for Canberra's Parliamentary Zone.

item.title: Aboriginal ownership of national parks celebrated at cultural festival
item.description: The Yuin people on the NSW South Coast celebrate two decades of Aboriginal ownership of two national parks.
item.dc:creator: Adriane Reardon

media:group.media:description: It's been 20 years since the handback of Biamanga and Gulaga National Parks to the Yuin people

item.title: Last Australian knocked out of French Open as Gauff's title defence ended
item.description: Aryna Sabalenka ends Australia's campaign at the French Open with a straightforward win over Daria Kasatkina, while Coco Gauff joins the slew of big names knocked out early in Paris.

media:group.media:description: Aryna Sabalenka beat Daria Kasatkina for her 100th victory as world number one.

item.title: The 'bread and butter' of a local court: More than 20 names on the traffic list
item.description: From hoons to drink drivers to people driving without a licence, one magistrate is trying to make sure the people brought before her know how quickly things can turn deadly on the roads.
item.dc:creator: Lottie Twyford

media:group.media:description: An artist's impression of a defendant brought before Magistrate Deborah Vasta.

item.title: Bioremediation of Agent Orange chemicals made in Sydney for Vietnam War
item.description: More than 50 years after the Vietnam War, students are working to remove Agent Orange remnants from a waterway connected to Sydney Harbour, where the chemicals were manufactured.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Corvo

media:group.media:description: Rubbish along Parramatta River near industrial sites.

item.title: Unlikely material behind Whitsundays artist's fashion collection
item.description: In the front yard of her home in the picturesque Whitsundays, Felicity Chapman strips the last pieces of flesh and sinew from a strip of barramundi skin to make wearable leather art.
item.dc:creator: Yasmine Wright Gittins
item.category: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

media:group.media:description: Felicity Chapman is an Aboriginal weaver and textile artist based in the Whitsundays.

item.title: PSG wins thrilling shootout for back-to-back Champions League crowns
item.description: Paris Saint-Germain beats Arsenal in a tense shootout in the Champions League final to be crowned European champions for the second straight year.

media:group.media:description: PSG's 2026 final was a much closer contest than the 5-0 victory over Inter last year.

item.title: As NZ cracks down on gang members, one pastor is trying to heal them
item.description: In a country where there now more gang members than police officers, a former gang member turned Christian pastor is trying to turn things around for others by addressing the root causes of gang life.
item.dc:creator: Marty Smiley

media:group.media:description: Lucky Te Koha, a former gang member turned Christian pastor, works with rival gang chapters around New Zealand.

item.title: Why are Australians still getting lost and dying in the outback?
item.description: Kelly’s son and ex-husband died "stupid" deaths in the Australian outback. She’s on a mission to make sure no more lives are lost.
item.dc:creator: Erin Parke

media:group.media:description: Kelly driving to the campsite where her son and ex husband died in the Murchison region of WA.

item.title: At 15 Mohamed Semra made headlines. At 27 he’s making history
item.description: At just 27, Mohamed Semra is not your average mayor. He overcame a childhood stutter and a viral moment in a Melbourne Apple Store to make political history – and he is just getting started.
item.dc:creator: Amos Roberts and Leisa Scott

media:group.media:description: Mohamed Semra was 27 when he was elected mayor of Maribyrnong City Council, in Melbourne, last year.

item.title: After nearly 80 years, the actors in this film have finally been understood
item.description: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated. Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.
item.dc:creator: Declan Durrant

media:group.media:description: The Robert Medley-painted poster for Bitter Springs, released in 1950.

item.title: Why 'irresponsible' dumping of mercury caused evacuations in outback town
item.description: An outback town's hospital, service station and school were affected when students discovered vials of mercury at the tip. Experts have explained why specialists have spent days doing testing in Cunnamulla.
item.dc:creator: Lachlan Ford

media:group.media:description: Responders have used atmospheric testers to detect mercury vapour.

item.title: How clay-court chaos is a return to the norm at the French Open
item.description: All the key favourites are now out of the men's draw at the French Open, but then again, this tournament has always been just a little bit different.
item.dc:creator: Simon Smale

media:group.media:description: Jannik Sinner is out, along with almost all the top seeds in the men's draw.

item.title: Periods stopped Vanessa participating in sport and she's not alone
item.description: Vanessa was forced to regularly skip sport because of her period. New research shows that more than 60 per cent of disabled athletes have done the same.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Wright

media:group.media:description: Period swimwear has changed the way Vanessa Ozols participates in training and competition.

item.title: Smoke engulfed their cities. Did it make their children sick?
item.description: Years after bushfires covered their cities in smoke, mothers are left wondering if their children's chronic illnesses began with what was in the air before they were born.
item.dc:creator: Jess Davis and Alex Lim

media:group.media:description: Six years after Black Summer parents and doctors face an unsettling question: What does bushfire smoke do to babies in the womb?

item.title: Generation AI: Schools in Asia are embracing artificial intelligence
item.description: Countries in Asia are emerging as the world's early adopters of AI in education, as the global race to lead in the technology accelerates.
item.dc:creator: Doug Dingwall

media:group.media:description: Schools in China are increasingly using and teaching AI as its government looks to build the country's prowess in artificial intelligence.

item.title: How Tiananmen Square vigils in Hong Kong have become unlawful
item.description: In May, Hong Kong's High Court began hearing closing arguments in the case of two democracy activists charged with inciting subversion for organising a candlelight vigil to remember Tiananmen Square.
item.dc:creator: Rudi Maxwell

media:group.media:description: Chow Hang-tung is on trial for inciting subversion but says what is truly on trial is Hong Kong law.

item.title: Police beat a woman in a Sydney street. Their own camera captured their cruelty
item.description: The family of a woman brutally assaulted by police in Sydney has fought to have body-worn camera vision released, in the hope officers will be better trained in responding to acute mental health situations.
item.dc:creator: Amy Greenbank, Elise Potaka, and Dylan Welch

media:group.media:description: An officer kicks Jodi Knott, in body-worn camera vision her family has fought to have released.

item.title: Women abandon football club after sex offender's return
item.description: When a man who sexually assaulted a minor on a post-season football trip was welcomed back to the club with open arms, Megan decided she was done.
item.dc:creator: Daniel Miles and Andy Burns

media:group.media:description: Megan Latham says she's taking a stand by speaking out.

item.title: As One Nation broadens its economic appeal, the Liberals return to culture wars
item.description: The Coalition's answer to the rise of conservative populism has been to treat it primarily as a cultural phenomenon. Under Angus Taylor, that instinct appears to be hardening.
item.dc:creator: Clare Armstrong

media:group.media:description: Angus Taylor is fighting One Nation where it is strongest rather than where it is vulnerable.

item.title: Is the government really overhauling employment services?
item.description: The government isn't rebuilding the Commonwealth Employment Service, but will its proposed changes fix a broken system?
item.dc:creator: Gareth Hutchens

media:group.media:description: Amanda Rishworth speaks about proposed changes to the employment services system at the National Press Club on May 27.

item.title: Former teachers' pets, rejoice. Guy Montgomery's spelling quiz is back
item.description: Welcome back to your Sunday morning quiz with everyone's favourite word wonderboy.

media:group.media:description: Guy Montgomery on the set of Guy Mont-Spelling Bee for season three.
